game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose!

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels with varied objectives (e.g., feeding characters, defeating bosses), collect health containers via exploration, earn extra lives through post-level minigames
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (health containers reset per level) balanced against exploration rewards; difficulty modes create fundamentally different experiences
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Buster Bunny in licensed Tiny Toon Adventures scenarios with character-specific challenges and familiar IP fan service
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Impressively varied level design with distinct objectives per stage rather than formulaic progression; tiered difficulty system that removes entire levels and restructures health economy; post-level minigame economy for extra lives tied to narrative wheel mechanic from source material

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives earned via post-level minigames; minigame type determined by spinning narrative wheel; health containers collected through exploration, reset each level
difficulty.continues_system: Japanese version: infinite continues (Normal) / Japanese version: varies by difficulty; US/EU version: 5 continues (Normal), 3 continues (Challenge/Hard)
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system implied by level-based progression with health reset between levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three distinct difficulty modes with structural changes: Children (easy mode with smaller levels, all 6 levels skipped in Japanese); Normal (full level design, increased health containers); Challenge/Hard (1 health container only, exploration-based recovery, limited continues)

progression.upgrade_system: Health container collection via exploration within levels; extra lives via minigame rewards
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression; higher difficulty modes unlock full ending and animated credits; Challenge mode unique to US/EU versions
replayability_hooks: Three distinct difficulty modes with structural variations
Password system for skipping to later levels (Japanese version has full passwords; US/EU restricted to Children difficulty)
Hidden level select accessible via cheat codes or forgotten button combo (Konami Code variant)
Minigame menu accessible via password entry (Elmyra, Shirley, Calamity) for practicing between-stage challenges
Different endings based on difficulty completion (Japanese: Babs vs Buster in rings; US/EU: completion messages encouraging next difficulty)
Animated credits only in Challenge mode (US/EU)

world_building_techniques: Licensed IP integration (Tiny Toon Adventures characters as level objectives)
Episode-referential mechanics (roulette wheel from 'The Wheel O'Comedy' episode used for minigame selection)
Character-specific scenarios (feed Dizzy Devil, defeat Duck Vader)
Cutscenes with region-specific localization (e.g., Back to the Future speedometer joke: km/h in JP, mph in US/EU)

boss_design.philosophy: Varied encounter types tied to character objectives rather than traditional boss gauntlet; thematic to level's narrative purpose

environmental_storytelling: School kitchen destruction by Dizzy Devil (environmental consequence of character behavior)
Level-specific character scenarios reflect cartoon narrative logic

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose! article
wikipedia - Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose! article
